title: Google supervisor favored White employees, lawsuit alleges
summary: An Illinois field service representative said his supervisor ignored requests for one-on-one meetings and a performance evaluation, excluded him from key meetings and more.

title: Unpaid ADA leave was reasonable for guide dog training, 6th Circuit says
summary: The appeals court determined the teacher could not point to any examples of nondisabled employees who were granted paid sick leave “even when the employee’s proposed absence did not qualify for that leave.”

title: HR must build better ERISA processes as class-action litigation ramps up, attorney says
summary: Vendor oversight and internal documentation are oft overlooked but key to mitigating risk, a Saul Ewing attorney told HR Dive.

title: Week in review: The Biden-era overtime rule is officially dead
summary: We’re rounding up last week’s stories, from recruiters managing a larger workload to a lack of speaking up at work.

title: DOL, Ed Dept tout reduced red tape for workforce development
summary: Stakeholders, however, say the Trump administration’s priorities lack a commitment to training.

title: How to develop high-potential employees
summary: Why do high-potential programs fail? It starts with strong individual performance. Find out how.

title: AI readiness is no longer optional for leadership teams
summary: AI adoption is accelerating, but many organizations are not ready to scale it responsibly.

title: Modernizing commute benefits for today’s flexible workforce
summary: With hybrid work reshaping commutes, flexible benefits help employees return with ease.

title: Revamped Colorado AI law targets ‘consequential’ HR decisions, takes effect in 2027
summary: After scrapping a version challenged in court, the state wants its second attempt to be “a model for the rest of the country,” Gov. Jared Polis said.

title: EEOC moves to axe EEO-1 reporting
summary: The agency wants to scrap a variety of employer reporting requirements, according to a plan sent to the White House Thursday.
